info.description:
StartFragment

# Notes Management App – API Tutorial

This tutorial explains how to use the **Notes Management App API** that supports **User Authentication (Signup/Login)** and **CRUD operations on Notes**. The API is documented using a Postman collection.

---

## 1\. Overview

The Notes Management App allows users to:

- Register and log in
    
- Create personal notes
    
- View all notes
    
- Update existing notes
    
- Delete notes
    

All note-related APIs are **protected** and require authentication.

---

## 2\. Tech Stack (Assumed)

- Backend: Node.js / Java (Spring Boot) _(based on API behavior)_
    
- Authentication: JWT (JSON Web Token)
    
- Database: MongoDB / MySQL
    
- API Testing Tool: Postman
    

---

## 3\. Authentication Flow

1. User signs up
    
2. User logs in
    
3. Server returns a JWT token
    
4. Token is sent in headers for all protected APIs
    

**Header Format:**

```
Authorization: Bearer <token>

 ```

---

## 4\. API Endpoints (As Implemented)

**Base URL:** `http://localhost:PORT`

Authentication is handled using **HTTP-only cookies** containing a JWT token (not Authorization headers).

---

### 4.1 Signup

**Endpoint:**

```
POST /auth/signup

 ```

**Request Body:**

``` json
{
  "name": "Archi Agrawal",
  "email": "test01@gmail.com",
  "password": "1234567890",
  "adminKey": "DFRPG7614R"
}

 ```

**Logic:**

- Checks if user already exists
    
- Hashes password using bcrypt
    
- Creates a welcome note automatically
    
- Stores JWT in cookie
    

**Response:**

``` json
{
  "status": true,
  "body": "SignUp Successfull"
}

 ```

---

### 4.2 Signin

**Endpoint:**

```
POST /auth/signin

 ```

**Request Body:**

``` json
{
  "email": "test01@gmail.com",
  "password": "1234567890"
}

 ```

**Response:**

``` json
{
  "status": true,
  "body": "Login Successfull"
}

 ```

> 

JWT token is stored in **HTTP-only cookie** named `token`.

 
  

---

### 4.3 Check Authentication

**Endpoint:**

```
GET /auth/checkAuth

 ```

**Purpose:**

- Verifies if user is already logged in
    

**Response:**

``` json
{
  "status": true,
  "body": "Already LoggedIn",
  "adminStatus": false
}

 ```

---

### 4.4 Signout

**Endpoint:**

```
POST /auth/signout

 ```

**Response:**

``` json
{
  "status": true,
  "body": "Log Out Successfull"
}

 ```

---

## 5\. Notes APIs (Protected)

All notes APIs require a valid `token` cookie.

---

### 5.1 Get All Notes

**Endpoint:**

```
GET /notes

 ```

**Response:**

``` json
{
  "status": true,
  "body": [
    {
      "_id": "noteId",
      "title": "My Note",
      "content": "Note content"
    }
  ]
}

 ```

---

### 5.2 Add Note

**Endpoint:**

```
PUT /notes

 ```

**Request Body:**

``` json
{
  "title": "New Note",
  "content": "Some content"
}

 ```

**Response:**

``` json
{
  "status": true,
  "body": {
    "title": "New Note",
    "content": "Some content"
  }
}

 ```

---

### 5.3 Get Single Note

**Endpoint:**

```
GET /notes/:noteId

 ```

---

### 5.4 Edit Note

**Endpoint:**

```
PATCH /notes/:noteId

 ```

**Request Body:**

``` json
{
  "title": "Updated Title",
  "content": "Updated Content"
}

 ```

---

### 5.5 Delete Note

**Endpoint:**

```
DELETE /notes/:noteId

 ```

**Response:**

``` json
{
  "status": true,
  "body": "Note Deleted Successfully"
}

 ```

---

## 6 Common Errors

| Status Code | Meaning |
| --- | --- |
| 400 | Bad Request |
| 401 | Unauthorized (Invalid Token) |
| 403 | Access Denied |
| 500 | Server Error |

---

## 7\. Conclusion

This documentation serves as a beginner-friendly tutorial and interview-ready explanation of a secure CRUD-based Notes Management application.

EndFragment
